waypoints.note: Embarked, Force S transports forming offshore north of Sword. Centroid in the run-in approach lane.

H-Hour Sword: 8 Brigade (South Lancs, East Yorks) lands on Queen White / Queen Red (La Brèche / Lion-sur-Mer area). 1st Special Service Brigade (Lord Lovat) and Free French Cdo Kieffer assault Ouistreham casino strongpoint at the Orne mouth.
Hermanville cleared ; 9 Brigade following through ; Ouistreham casino taken by Kieffer's commandos.
Lord Lovat's commandos cross to Pegasus Bridge linking up with 6th Airborne. Main division pushing south through Beuville and Periers-sur-le-Dan.

disputedBy.claim: Whether Caen was a realistic D-Day objective for 3rd ID is one of the most-disputed questions of the campaign. Montgomery's plan called for Caen on D-Day ; Harrison concludes the schedule was unrealistic given the depth of advance and the 21. Panzer counterattack ; Beevor (cross-check) pushes back on this revisionism, noting at least 7 hours' delay before 9 Brigade was committed inland.

waypoints.note: Lead Sherman Fireflies of Staffordshire Yeomanry engage 21. Panzer's Pz-Rgt 22 at Bieville. Division blocked from Caen by the German counterattack.

End of MVP window: 3rd ID line stops at the Lebisey ridge, ~6 km short of Caen. The objective Montgomery had set for D-Day will not fall for another month. Position is approximate divisional centroid.
